summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a bare CR (0x0D) character in the EHLO host argument is preserved through the SMTP command processing pipeline and embedded in the Received header of the resulting email message, creating a header injection condition verifiable in the captured message stream.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a bare CR (0x0D) character in the EHLO host argument survives sanitization and is embedded in the Received header of the captured email message. A downstream email parser that treats bare CR as a line break would interpret the injected content as a separate email header.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction starts Haraka from the vulnerable source, connects via SMTP with a crafted EHLO containing a bare CR (0x0D) in the host argument, completes a mail transaction, and the captured message file contains the byte sequence 0x0D followed by the injection marker 'X-EvohuntPoC:' within the Received header, confirming header injection.
verification_method: The verification script checks the captured message file for the presence of the byte sequence \rX-EvohuntPoC: (0x0D followed by the ASCII string X-EvohuntPoC:) which confirms that the bare CR from the attacker-controlled EHLO argument was preserved and embedded in the Received header without sanitization.
evidence_produced_by_run: The captured message file (/tmp/captured_message.eml) contains the byte 0x0D (bare CR) followed by 'X-EvohuntPoC:' within the Received header, confirming the attacker-controlled EHLO argument was embedded without sanitization.
The Haraka server accepted the crafted EHLO command and completed the mail transaction without error, demonstrating that the vulnerability is reachable without special conditions or authentication.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Haraka repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
